text.title: PWA Development Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the PWA Development methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: manifest.webmanifest MUST declare `name`, `short_name`, `start_url`, `display`, `theme_color`, and `icons` including 192x192 + 512x512 + maskable variant.

rationale.source: W3C web app manifest spec; Lighthouse PWA audit

rule.rationale: Missing icons or display fail the install prompt; mobile browsers silently refuse to show the A2HS banner.

rule.statement: Service worker MUST be registered at root scope (`/`) and scope MUST be declared explicitly in registration; nested scopes are forbidden unless documented.

rationale.source: Service Worker spec; cross-scope cache leak postmortems

rule.rationale: Nested scopes cause requests outside the SW to bypass cache silently; root scope is the safe default.

rule.statement: Application shell (`index.html`, JS entry) MUST NOT use `cache-first`; use `network-first` with timeout fallback OR `stale-while-revalidate` with explicit revalidation.
rule.rationale: Cache-first on the shell freezes users on the deploy version they first loaded; no new code reaches them.

rule.statement: SW MUST call `skipWaiting()` + `clients.claim()` and the UI MUST show a user-visible reload prompt when a new SW activates; silent updates are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without skipWaiting, the new SW sleeps until all tabs close; without a reload prompt, users stay on the old assets indefinitely.

rule.statement: An `/offline.html` page MUST be precached and served when network fails for navigation requests; opaque blank screens on offline are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without an offline page, navigation requests offline return browser-default error pages, hurting the installed-app feel.

rule.statement: Each route in the caching plan MUST carry an explicit TTL (max-age) and `maxEntries` cap; unbounded caches are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Unbounded caches grow until quota errors; explicit TTL keeps the cache shape predictable.

rule.statement: SW registration MUST be guarded by HTTPS check (or localhost dev); HTTP registration must not be attempted in production.
rule.rationale: SW only runs in a secure context; attempting HTTP registration in production fails silently and breaks the install path.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
